message:
fix(tests,runtime,widgets): cleanup resource leaks, suppress warnings, and simplify word-delete

seed_rpc_integration.rs (doctor_frankentui):
- Fix ServerHarness::drop to properly handle TCP wake-up connection
  failures: wrap TcpStream::connect in if-let and explicitly call
  shutdown(Both) on success before joining the server thread. Previously
  the unused TcpStream was silently dropped without shutdown, and connect
  failures were not handled, which could leave the server thread hanging
  in tests.

retry.rs (ftui-runtime):
- Bind spawned thread handles to _handle variables in spawn_timeout,
  spawn_timeout_named, and retry_with_timeout to suppress unused
  JoinHandle warnings. The handles are intentionally not joined (the
  functions use channel-based timeout instead), but binding them
  documents the intent and silences clippy/compiler diagnostics.

input.rs (ftui-widgets):
- Simplify delete_word_backward by reusing the existing
  move_cursor_word_left logic instead of reimplementing word boundary
  detection inline. The old implementation duplicated the grapheme class
  scanning logic and had a subtle behavioral difference: it only skipped
  trailing whitespace OR deleted one token class, whereas
  move_cursor_word_left handles the full word-left motion. This
  consolidation ensures delete-word-backward and cursor-word-left agree
  on word boundaries.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
